title: ChatGPT Canvas enters stable release
description: OpenAI moved ChatGPT Canvas from preview to general availability for web and desktop, adding persistent side-by-side editing and inline model suggestions.

title: OpenAI Academy global expansion
description: OpenAI expanded Academy cohorts to additional countries and launched structured enterprise and educator tracks with certification pathways.

title: ChatGPT Projects organization upgrades
description: Projects added nested folders, smarter source deduplication, and cross-project file references to support larger research and engineering workflows.

title: ChatGPT Health
description: OpenAI introduced a dedicated Health space in ChatGPT with purpose-built encryption, isolated memory, and conversation siloing — health data not used to train models or shared with other chats.

title: ChatGPT Go tier and ads
description: OpenAI announced ad testing in ChatGPT for Free and Go users in the US while keeping paid tiers ad-free, as part of its official expansion and access strategy.

title: More visual ChatGPT responses
description: Responses for everyday questions gained at-a-glance visuals (stats, unit conversions, calculations) and tap-to-expand highlights for key people, places, and products with sourced side panels. Available on iOS, Android, and web.

title: Codex app for macOS
description: OpenAI launched the Codex desktop app as a command center for supervising multiple long-running agents in parallel. Available to Plus, Pro, Business, Enterprise, and Edu plans on Apple Silicon (macOS 14+).

title: GPT-5.3-Codex
description: OpenAI released GPT-5.3-Codex as its most capable agentic coding model, combining coding, reasoning, and computer-use strength. First OpenAI model rated "High" cybersecurity capability under its Preparedness Framework.

title: Ads go live in ChatGPT Free and Go
description: Advertising officially rolled out in ChatGPT for Free and Go tier users in the US, following the January announcement period.

title: GPT-5.3-Codex-Spark
description: Ultra-fast real-time coding model running on Cerebras Wafer Scale Engine 3 at 1000+ tokens/second. ChatGPT Pro research preview — first OpenAI model on dedicated Cerebras low-latency hardware.

title: Projects can add sources from apps and chats
description: ChatGPT Projects gained app links, saved chat outputs, and ad-hoc text as reusable, always-in-context project sources.

title: GPT-5.4
description: New mainline flagship across ChatGPT, Codex, and API: 1M token context, Codex-level coding, 75% on OSWorld computer use (exceeding human baseline at 72.4%), and 33% fewer per-claim errors vs GPT-5.2.

title: GPT-5.4 mini and nano
description: Faster, cheaper variants: mini (400k context, $0.75/$4.50 per MTok) and nano (API only, $0.20/$1.25 per MTok). Free-tier ChatGPT upgraded to GPT-5.4 mini; mini available in Codex for subagent parallelism.

title: File Library in ChatGPT
description: ChatGPT began saving uploaded and model-generated files into a persistent File Library, reusable across chats without re-uploading.

title: ChatGPT on Apple CarPlay
description: OpenAI launched ChatGPT in Apple CarPlay, enabling fully hands-free voice conversations for drivers on iOS 26.4 or later with a supported car.

title: Codex for (almost) everything
description: OpenAI released a major Codex update spanning computer use, image generation with gpt-image-1.5, 90+ new plugins, richer PR review workflows, SSH devbox access (alpha), and automation/memory improvements in the desktop app.

title: ChatGPT Images 2.0 with legible text rendering
description: OpenAI rolled out Images 2.0 to all ChatGPT and Codex users, with dramatically improved legible text (including Japanese, Korean, Hindi, and Bengali), web-aware "thinking" for multi-image prompts and comic strips, and output up to 2K resolution; a gpt-image-2 API also launched, priced by quality and resolution.

title: OpenAI × Infosys: Codex and Topaz integration
description: OpenAI announced a partnership with Infosys to embed Codex and other OpenAI tools into the Topaz AI platform, targeting software engineering, legacy system upgrades, and DevOps across Infosys's 60+ countries of enterprise customers. The deal is part of OpenAI's newly announced Codex Labs initiative alongside Accenture, Capgemini, CGI, Cognizant, PwC, and TCS.

title: OpenAI releases GPT-5.5
description: OpenAI launched GPT-5.5, described as its "smartest and most intuitive" model, framed as progress toward a unified ChatGPT + Codex + AI-browser "super app." Greg Brockman said it's a faster, sharper thinker that uses fewer tokens than GPT-5.4; OpenAI claims benchmark wins over Gemini 3.1 Pro and Claude Opus 4.5. Rollout: Plus, Pro, Business, and Enterprise; GPT-5.5 Pro limited to Pro, Business, Enterprise.
